description: ESR v0.2 thread-scoped Feishu handler. One actor per Feishu thread bound to a CC session: dedups inbound msg_ids, captures chat_id on first inbound, notifies the esr-channel session on msg_received (replacing the v0.1 tmux send-keys path), and relays cc_output back to Feishu as send_message. Pure function — no on_spawn lifecycle hook (PRD 05 F14).

key_symbols.name: on_msg
key_symbols.description: @handler(actor_type='feishu_thread_proxy', name='on_msg') — dispatches on event.event_type to _handle_inbound / _handle_outbound; unknown events return (state, []).

key_symbols.name: _handle_inbound
key_symbols.description: Handles 'msg_received': dedup check on message_id, captures chat_id on first inbound, emits Emit(adapter='esr-channel', action='notify_session', args={session_id=thread_id, source='feishu', chat_id, message_id, user, content}).

key_symbols.name: _handle_outbound
key_symbols.description: Handles 'cc_output': if chat_id is set, emits Emit(adapter='feishu', action='send_message', args={chat_id, content=text}); silently drops if chat_id is unset (orphan cc_output before first inbound).

key_symbols.name: _DEDUP_CAP
key_symbols.description: Hard cap of 1000 on dedup set size (PRD 05 F11).

key_symbols.name: FeishuThreadState
key_symbols.description: @handler_state(actor_type='feishu_thread_proxy', schema_version=1); frozen pydantic BaseModel. Fields: thread_id:str='' (set at spawn via pattern init_directive), chat_id:str='' (captured on first inbound), dedup:frozenset[str]=frozenset(), dedup_order:tuple[str,...]=() (insertion order for FIFO eviction), ack_msg_id:str|None=None.

key_symbols.name: with_added_dedup
key_symbols.description: Return new state with msg_id appended to dedup + dedup_order; evicts the oldest entry once len exceeds _DEDUP_CAP (=1000).

key_symbols.name: with_chat_id
key_symbols.description: Return new state with chat_id field updated — only called on first inbound.

key_symbols.name: with_ack
key_symbols.description: Return new state with ack_msg_id set (informational, currently unused by on_msg).

key_symbols.name: _inbound
key_symbols.description: Test helper building msg_received Event from adapter/feishu with message_id/chat_id/content/sender_id.

key_symbols.name: _cc_output
key_symbols.description: Test helper building cc_output Event from adapter/cc_tmux.

key_symbols.name: test_inbound_emits_notify_session
key_symbols.description: PRD 05 F12 — inbound msg_received emits a single Emit to adapter='esr-channel' action='notify_session' with session_id=thread_id; state gets dedup entry and captured chat_id.

key_symbols.name: test_duplicate_inbound_produces_no_actions
key_symbols.description: PRD 05 F11 — re-delivered msg_id (already in dedup) yields actions==[] and state dedup unchanged.

key_symbols.name: test_cc_output_emits_send_message_to_feishu
key_symbols.description: PRD 05 F13 — cc_output with chat_id emits Emit(adapter='feishu', action='send_message', chat_id=..., content=text).

key_symbols.name: test_cc_output_without_chat_id_is_silent
key_symbols.description: Orphan cc_output before any inbound (no chat_id) returns (state, []) without error.

key_symbols.name: test_no_on_spawn_symbol_exported
key_symbols.description: PRD 05 F14 — pure-function model has no lifecycle callback; module has no on_spawn attr.

key_symbols.name: test_arbitrary_event_ignored
key_symbols.description: Unknown event_type returns (state, []) — default dispatch branch.

key_symbols.name: test_state_has_defaults
key_symbols.description: Default FeishuThreadState has thread_id='', dedup=frozenset(), ack_msg_id=None, chat_id=''.

key_symbols.name: test_state_is_frozen
key_symbols.description: Mutating thread_id raises pydantic ValidationError (frozen=True).

key_symbols.name: test_with_added_dedup_adds
key_symbols.description: with_added_dedup returns new instance containing msg_id; original unchanged.

key_symbols.name: test_dedup_bounded_to_1000
key_symbols.description: PRD 05 F11 — after inserting 1001 distinct msg_ids, len(dedup)==1000, oldest ('m0') dropped, newest ('m1000') retained.

key_symbols.name: test_with_chat_id
key_symbols.description: with_chat_id returns new instance with chat_id set; original unchanged.

interfaces.name: on_msg(state: FeishuThreadState, event: Event) -> (FeishuThreadState, list[Action])
interfaces.description: Registered handler for actor_type='feishu_thread_proxy'. Handled event_types: 'msg_received' (from feishu adapter), 'cc_output' (from cc_tmux adapter). Emitted actions: Emit(adapter='esr-channel', action='notify_session') on inbound; Emit(adapter='feishu', action='send_message') on outbound.

interfaces.name: FeishuThreadState
interfaces.description: Frozen pydantic state model for a feishu_thread_proxy actor. Fields: thread_id, chat_id, dedup (frozenset capped at 1000), dedup_order (FIFO tuple for eviction), ack_msg_id. Immutable-update helpers: with_added_dedup, with_chat_id, with_ack.

user_flows.name: Inbound Feishu message → notify CC session (v0.2 §3.3 primary path)
steps: Feishu adapter receives a webhook and emits Event(event_type='msg_received', args={message_id, chat_id, sender_id, content}).
on_msg dedup-checks message_id against state.dedup; if already seen returns (state, []).
New msg_id: adds it to dedup (FIFO eviction at 1000); if chat_id was empty, captures it.

Emits Emit(adapter='esr-channel', action='notify_session', args={session_id=thread_id, source='feishu', chat_id, message_id, user=sender_id, content}) — PeerServer short-circuits this via SessionRegistry to the bound CC session.

user_flows.name: CC output → relay to Feishu chat (PRD 05 F13)
steps: cc_tmux adapter captures CC stdout and emits Event(event_type='cc_output', args={text, session}).
on_msg: if state.chat_id is unset (no prior inbound), returns (state, []) silently.
Otherwise emits Emit(adapter='feishu', action='send_message', args={chat_id=state.chat_id, content=text}).

notes: v0.2 rewrite (commit context, on_msg.py docstring): v0.1 matched a non-existent 'feishu_msg_received' event and emitted tmux send-keys; both were dead code because the adapter actually emits 'msg_received'. The rewrite aligns the event name and switches the primary inbound path to the synthetic 'esr-channel' adapter + notify_session action that PeerServer resolves via SessionRegistry. The outbound cc_output→send_message branch is retained for the scenario mock path. Difference from handler-feishu-app (app-scoped): feishu_app handles whole-app lifecycle events (bot-join, app-level routing, spawning thread actors) while feishu_thread handles exactly one bound (thread_id ↔ chat_id ↔ CC session) conversation — it owns per-thread dedup and the chat_id learned at first inbound, and never spawns more actors. thread_id is set at spawn time by the pattern's init_directive (per feishu-thread-session pattern, see py/tests/test_pattern_feishu_thread_session.py) and never mutates thereafter; chat_id is write-once via with_chat_id on first inbound. State is fully frozen; all updates go through model_copy helpers. ack_msg_id field and with_ack helper exist on state but are not currently wired into on_msg — reserved for a future ack branch. Handler is pure (no on_spawn, empty allowed_imports in esr.toml) and covered by 11 passing unit tests.
